method_atom: A systematic asymptotic expansion in the inverse of a large number of internal components (a 1/N expansion) of a many-component interacting field defined on a discretized d-dimensional lattice, carried to next-to-leading order to compute two-point correlation functions and a normalized fourth-order-to-second-order moment ratio (a dimensionless cumulant diagnosing collective behavior). A dynamically generated gap emerges self-consistently and acts as a coupling-dependent long-wavelength regulator that vanishes as the lattice grows without bound; the nontrivial cancellation of the resulting long-wavelength divergences in symmetry-invariant observables is verified explicitly, and a finite infinite-size (thermodynamic) limit is extracted, cross-checked against stochastic sampling.
object_atom: Noncompact SO(1,N)-symmetric sigma-models — fields valued in a hyperbolic (noncompact) symmetric space — formulated on a lattice in dimensions d >= 2, with their spin and current two-point functions and Binder cumulant, and the questions of criticality and triviality in the SO(1,N)-invariant sector.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the large-parameter expansion plus self-generated regulator, divergence-cancellation, and thermodynamic-limit/cumulant analysis; the domain-specific object is the noncompact SO(1,N) lattice sigma-model and its invariant correlators, with the noncompact-symmetry structure being the main hard-to-fully-separate feature.
